IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: THE ORGANIZATION'S MISSION IS TO CONSERVE WILDLIFE AND WILDERNESS LANDS; TO EDUCATE THE YOUTH AND THE GENERAL PUBLIC AND TO PROMOTE AND PROTECT THE RIGHTS AND INTERESTS OF HUNTERS WORLDWIDE.
IRS990.MissionDesc: THE ORGANIZATION'S MISSION IS TO CONSERVE WILDLIFE AND WILDERNESS LANDS; TO EDUCATE THE YOUTH AND THE GENERAL PUBLIC AND TO PROMOTE AND PROTECT THE RIGHTS AND INTERESTS OF HUNTERS WORLDWIDE.
IRS990.Desc: THE ORGANIZATION PUBLISHES THE FOLLOWING PUBLICATIONS SOLELY FOR THE ORGANIZATION'S MEMBERSHIP: GAME TRAILS MAGAZINE WHICH IS PUBLISHED QUARTERLY AND THE ORGANIZATION'S ONLINE NEWSLETTER.

ProgSrvcAccomActy2Grp.Desc: THE ORGANIZATION PROVIDES GRANTS TO FURTHER ITS MISSION OF WILDLIFE CONSERVATION, EDUCATION AND HUNTER ADVOCACY. APPROXIMATELY 44% OF THE GRANTS GO TOWARD CONSERVATION, 36% FOR EDUCATION AND 20% FOR HUNTER ADVOCACY. ABOUT TWO THIRD OF THE GRANTS SUPPORT PROJECTS BEYOND TEXAS BORDERS. EXAMPLES INCLUDE CONSERVATION INITIATIVES FOR LIONS, ELEPHANTS, GIRAFFES, AND LEOPARDS, DEVELOPING CONSERVATION STRATEGIES IN CANADA, AND ADVOCATING FOR HUNTER INTERESTS IN WASHINGTON D.C. THE REMAINDER OF THE GRANTS SUPPORT TEXAS INITIATIVES SUCH AS DESERT BIGHORN SHEEP RESTORATION, QUAIL HABITAT IMPROVEMENTS, MULE DEER RESEARCH AND EXPANDING CONSERVATION CURRICULUM.

ProgSrvcAccomActy3Grp.Desc: PROGRAM SERVICES FOR MEMBERS INCLUDING MONTHLY MEETINGS, GUEST SPEAKERS, COMPETITION EVENTS AND OPEN HOUSES.

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE ENDOWMENT FUNDS ARE INTENDED TO SUPPORT THE LONG-TERM SUSTAINABILITY OF OUR MISSION BY ENSURING WE CAN CONTINUE MAKING AN IMPACT FOR YEARS TO COME. THE USE OF THESE FUNDS ALIGNS WITH DONOR INTENT AND THE ORGANIZATION'S STRATEGIC GOALS, WITH ANY FUTURE DISTRIBUTIONS MANAGED IN ACCORDANCE WITH OUR BYLAWS. NO FUNDS HAVE YET BEEN USED AS THERE IS A LEVEL THAT MUST BE REACHED BEFORE EARNINGS ONLY WILL BE ALLOWED TO BE DISBURSED.
DIRECT FUNDRAISING EXPENSES, REPORTED ON PART VIII LINE 8B 3,674,789. COST OF GOODS SOLD, REPORTED ON PART VIII LINE 10B 107,991. ADVERTISING EXPENSES, NETTED WITH PROGRAM SERVICE REVENUE 30,035.
DIRECT FUNDRAISING EXPENSES, REPORTED ON PART VIII LINE 8B 3,674,789. COST OF GOODS SOLD, REPORTED ON PART VIII LINE 10B 107,991. ADVERTISING EXPENSES, NETTED WITH PROGRAM SERVICE REVENUE 30,035.

GrantsToOrgOutsideUSGrp.RegionTxt: NORTH AMERICA - CANADA AND MEXICO, BUT NOT THE UNITED STATES
NORTH AMERICA - CANADA AND MEXICO, BUT NOT THE UNITED STATES

USAddress.AddressLine1Txt: 3240 S I-10 SERVICE RD W STE 200

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: PART I, LINE 2, PROCEDURES FOR MONITORING THE USE OF GRANT FUNDS
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE DALLAS SAFARI CLUB'S MISSION IS TO CONSERVE WILDLIFE AND WILDERNESS LANDS; TO EDUCATE YOUTH AND THE GENERAL PUBLIC AND TO PROMOTE AND PROTECT THE RIGHTS AND INTERESTS OF HUNTERS WORLDWIDE. AS PART OF THAT MISSION, THE DALLAS SAFARI CLUB OFFERS GRANT/FINANCIAL SUPPORT TO CAUSES IN THE USA AND ABROAD TO THOSE WHO SHARE THE SAME MISSION AND GOALS. THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS APPROVE ALL GRANTS AND MONITOR HOW THE GRANT FUNDS ARE USED. ORGANIZATIONS RECEIVING GRANTS PERIODICALLY REPORT BACK TO THE DALLAS SAFARI CLUB REGARDING THEIR USE OF THE FUNDS RECEIVED.

THE BONUS PLAN FOR THE EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR AND STAFF IS BASED ON CERTAIN REVENUE TARGETS AND EXPENSE SAVINGS. THE BONUS PLAN FOR EACH YEAR IS APPROVED BY THE ADMINISTRATIVE/COMPENSATION COMMITTEE AND SUBMITTED AND APPROVED BY THE TREASURER AND THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 11B
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE TAX RETURN IS PREPARED BY AN INDEPENDENT CPA FIRM, REVIEWED AND SIGNED BY THE TREASURER AND SUBMITTED TO THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS FOR REVIEW AND QUESTIONS PRIOR TO FILING.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 12C
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS MEET ONCE PER MONTH AND ARE RESPONSIBLE FOR MONITORING AND ENFORCING COMPLIANCE WITH THE CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 15
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE PROCESS FOR DETERMINING COMPENSATION INCLUDES USE OF OUTSIDE MARKET EVALUATION FIRM, THEN RECOMMENDATIONS ARE REVIEWED BY THE ADMIN COMMITTEE.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION C, LINE 19
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: ALL GOVERNING DOCUMENTS ARE MADE AVAILABLE TO THE PUBLIC UPON REQUEST.
